heading: Right to counsel
label: 1362 Right to counsel
text: In any removal proceedings before an immigration judge and in any appeal proceedings before the Attorney General from any such removal proceedings, the person concerned shall have the privilege of being represented (at no expense to the Government) by such counsel, authorized to practice in such proceedings, as he shall choose.
notes: Amendments1996—, substituted “an immigration judge” for “a special inquiry officer”.Pub. L. 104–208, § 371(b)(9), substituted “removal” for “exclusion or deportation” in two places.Pub. L. 104–208, § 308(d)(4)(O)
